cot_prefix: Okay, let's find out which of the choices is correct. First, I'll read the passage and then go through each option one by one to check which one is true. The passage says Yves Gomes is a university student in Maryland and is fighting deportation to India. He was born in the US, as he says he was brought to India when he was 14 months old. That means he was born in the US, which makes him one of the undocumented immigrants brought to the US before 16. He left the US when he was 14 months old, but he grew up in the US until then? Or did he go to India when he was 14 months old? Wait, the passage says he left India with his parents when he was 14 months old. Wait, maybe I misread. Let me check again. Actually, the text says: "He is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was just 14 months old. They were deported more than a year ago." So he was in India when he was 14 months old, and then left India with his parents. Wait, that would mean he was born in India? But he says he wants to study in the US and go to medical school here. But if he was born in India, then maybe he was brought to the US as an infant. Wait, the passage says he is an illegal immigrant in the US. So perhaps he was born in India, but came to the US when he was 14 months old, and his parents were deported more than a year ago. Wait, the sentence is a bit confusing. Let me parse it again. The sentence says: "He is fighting deportation to India, a country he left with his parents when he was just 14 months old. They were deported more than a year ago." So "they" refers to his parents. So his parents were deported more than a year ago. And he left India when he was 14 months old with his parents.
